This document describes the error response format, error codes, and performance characteristics.\n6\t\n7\t## Error Response Format\n8\t\n9\tAll error responses follow the S3 XML error format with consistent headers:\n10\t\n11\t### HTTP Headers\n12\t\n13\t```http\n14\tContent-Type: application/xml\n15\tStatus: 403 Forbidden (for authentication errors)\n16\t```\n17\t\n18\t### Response Body\n19\t\n20\t```xml\n21\t<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n22\t<Error>\n23\t  <Code>ErrorCode</Code>\n24\t  <Message>Human-readable error description</Message>\n25\t</Error>\n26\t```\n27\t\n28\t## Authentication Error Codes\n29\t\n30\t### Invalid Credentials\n31\t\n32\t| Error Code | HTTP Status | Message | When Returned |\n33\t|------------|-------------|---------|---------------|\n34\t| `InvalidAccessKeyId` | 403 | The AWS Access Key Id you provided does not exist in our records. | Access key not found in credentials store |\n35\t| `SignatureDoesNotMatch` | 403 | The request signature we calculated does not match the signature you provided. | Secret key is incorrect or signature is invalid |\n36\t| `MissingAuthenticationToken` | 403 | Missing authentication token. | Authorization header is completely missing |\n37\t| `InvalidCredential` | 403 | Invalid credential format. | Credential string has insufficient parts (expected 5 parts) |\n38\t\n39\t### Malformed Authorization Headers\n40\t\n41\t| Error Code | HTTP Status | Message | When Returned |\n42\t|------------|-------------|---------|---------------|\n43\t| `IncompleteSignature` | 403 | The authorization header is malformed. | Required components missing (Credential, SignedHeaders, or Signature) |\n44\t| `InvalidAlgorithm` | 403 | The authorization header algorithm is not supported. | Algorithm is not AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 |\n45\t| `AccessDenied` | 403 | Access Denied. | Generic authentication failure |\n46\t\n47\t### Timing Issues\n48\t\n49\t| Error Code | HTTP Status | Message | When Returned |\n50\t|------------|-------------|---------|---------------|\n51\t| `RequestExpired` | 403 | Request has expired. | Request timestamp is outside the allowed window (±15 minutes) |\n52\t| `MissingDateHeader` | 403 | Missing required header: x-amz-date. | X-Amz-Date header is missing |\n53\t\n54\t## Malformed Signature Scenarios\n55\t\n56\tARMOR validates signature format and provides specific error codes:\n57\t\n58\t| Scenario | Error Code | Performance |\n59\t|----------|------------|-------------|\n60\t| Non-hex signature characters | `SignatureDoesNotMatch` | < 50ms |\n61\t| Too short signature (< 32 bytes) | `SignatureDoesNotMatch` | < 50ms |\n62\t| Empty signature | `IncompleteSignature` | < 50ms |\n63\t| Random characters in signature | `SignatureDoesNotMatch` | < 50ms |\n64\t\n65\t## Performance Guarantees\n66\t\n67\tAll authentication rejection scenarios respond within strict time limits:\n68\t\n69\t| Test Type | Target | Actual | Environment |\n70\t|-----------|--------|--------|-------------|\n71\t| Unit test rejections | < 100ms | < 1ms | Local httptest |\n72\t| Integration test rejections | < 500ms | < 50ms | Real server |\n73\t| Malformed signature rejections | < 50ms | < 1ms | Local httptest |\n74\t\n75\t### Performance Test Coverage\n76\t\n77\tThe test suite includes performance verification for all rejection scenarios:\n78\t\n79\t1. **TestInvalidCredentialRejection/Rejection_happens_quickly** - Verifies < 100ms response time\n80\t2. **TestMalformedSignatureRejection/Rejection_happens_quickly** - Verifies < 50ms response time for malformed signatures\n81\t\n82\t## Error Message Quality\n83\t\n84\tAll error responses include:\n85\t\n86\t1. **Specific Error Code** - Identifies the exact problem (e.g., `InvalidAccessKeyId`, `SignatureDoesNotMatch`)\n87\t2. **Meaningful Message** - Human-readable description of the problem\n88\t3. **XML Format** - S3-compatible XML structure with proper escaping\n89\t\n90\t### Message Validation\n91\t\n92\tTest suite verifies that:\n93\t- Error messages are never empty\n94\t- Messages are at least 10 characters long\n95\t- Messages contain relevant keywords (authentication, signature, credential, algorithm, header, aws4)\n96\t- XML is properly escaped to prevent injection\n97\t\n98\t## Response Consistency\n99\t\n100\tAll error responses maintain consistency:\n101\t\n102\t### Headers\n103\t- Always return `Content-Type: application/xml`\n104\t- Always return appropriate HTTP status code (403 for auth errors)\n105\t- Response body is never empty\n106\t\n107\t### Structure\n108\t- XML declaration with encoding: `<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`\n109\t- Root element: `<Error>`\n110\t- Two child elements: `<Code>` and `<Message>`\n111\t- Proper XML escaping for special characters\n112\t\n113\t## Test Coverage Summary\n114\t\n115\tThe ARMOR test suite includes comprehensive coverage for rejection scenarios:\n116\t\n117\t### Unit Tests (`invalid_credential_test.go`)\n118\t- 12 test scenarios covering:\n119\t  - Invalid AWS credentials\n120\t  - Malformed signatures  \n121\t  - Missing authentication headers\n122\t  - Malformed authorization headers\n123\t  - Insufficient credential parts\n124\t  - Missing required components (SignedHeaders, Signature, date)\n125\t  - Expired requests\n126\t  - Performance validation\n127\t\n128\t### Unit Tests (`malformed_signature_test.go`)\n129\t- 20+ test scenarios covering:\n130\t  - Garbage signature strings (non-hex, too short, empty, random chars)\n131\t  - Invalid signature formats (missing algorithm, wrong algorithm, missing components)\n132\t  - Partial signatures (missing components)\n133\t  - Error message quality\n134\t  - Performance validation\n135\t\n136\t### Integration Tests (`invalid_credential_integration_test.go`)\n137\t- Real server tests with actual HTTP client\n138\t- Performance validation under realistic conditions\n139\t- End-to-end verification of error responses\n140\t\n141\t### Headers Consistency (`error_response_test.go`)\n142\t- Verifies consistent headers across all rejection types\n143\t- Validates Content-Type header\n144\t- Ensures proper XML structure\n145\t\n146\t## Examples\n147\t\n148\t### Example 1: Invalid Access Key\n149\t\n150\t**Request:**\n151\t```http\n152\tGET /test-bucket/test-key HTTP/1.1\n153\tHost: test-bucket.s3.us-east-005.backblazeb2.com\n154\tAuthorization: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=INVALIDKEY/20250714/us-east-005/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=abc123...\n155\tX-Amz-Date: 20250714T044805Z\n156\t```\n157\t\n158\t**Response:**\n159\t```http\n160\tHTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden\n161\tContent-Type: application/xml\n162\t\n163\t<?xml version=\"1.0\" test-bucket.s3.us-east-005.backblazeb2.com\n176\t```\n177\t\n178\t**Response:**\n179\t```http\n180\tHTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden\n181\tContent-Type: application/xml\n182\t\n183\t<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n184\t<Error>\n185\t  <Code>MissingAuthenticationToken</Code>\n186\t  <Message>Missing authentication token.</Message>\n187\t</Error>\n188\t```\n189\t\n190\t### Example 3: Malformed Signature\n191\t\n192\t**Request:**\n193\t```http\n194\tGET /test-bucket/test-key HTTP/1.1\n195\tHost: test-bucket.s3.us-east-005.backblazeb2.com\n196\tAuthorization: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20250714/us-east-005/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host, Signature=!@#$%^&*()\n197\tX-Amz-Date: 20250714T044805Z\n198\t```\n199\t\n200\t**Response:**\n201\t```http\n202\tHTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden\n203\tContent-Type: application/xml\n204\t\n205\t<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n206\t<Error>\n207\t  <Code>SignatureDoesNotMatch</Code>\n208\t  <Message>The request signature we calculated does not match the signature you provided.</Message>\n209\t</Error>\n210\t```\n211\t\n212\t## Implementation\n213\t\n214\tError responses are generated by two implementations:\n215\t\n216\t1. **Server Handler** (`internal/server/server.go:796-805`)\n217\t   ```go\n218\t   func (s *Server) writeError(w http.ResponseWriter, code, message string, statusCode int) {\n219\t       w.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n220\t       w.WriteHeader(statusCode)\n221\t       // XML generation with proper escaping\n222\t   }\n223\t   ```\n224\t\n225\t2. **Handlers Package** (`internal/server/handlers/handlers.go:2695-2704`)\n226\t   ```go\n227\t   func (h *Handlers) writeError(w http.ResponseWriter, code, message string, statusCode int) {\n228\t       w.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n229\t       w.WriteHeader(statusCode)\n230\t       // XML generation with proper escaping\n231\t   }\n232\t   ```\n233\t\n234\tBoth implementations ensure:\n235\t- Consistent Content-Type header\n236\t- Proper XML escaping to prevent injection\n237\t- S3-compatible format\n238\t\n239\t## Testing\n240\t\n241\tTo run the error ARMOR Error Response Documentation\n\n## Overview\n\nARMOR provides S3-compatible error responses for all authentication and request failures. This document describes the error response format, error codes, and performance characteristics.\n\n## Error Response Format\n\nAll error responses follow the S3 XML error format with consistent headers:\n\n### HTTP Headers\n\n```http\nContent-Type: application/xml\nStatus: 403 Forbidden (for authentication errors)\n```\n\n### Response Body\n\n```xml\n<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>ErrorCode</Code>\n  <Message>Human-readable error description</Message>\n</Error>\n```\n\n## Authentication Error Codes\n\n### Invalid Credentials\n\n| Error Code | HTTP Status | Message | When Returned |\n|------------|-------------|---------|---------------|\n| `InvalidAccessKeyId` | 403 | The AWS Access Key Id you provided does not exist in our records. | Access key not found in credentials store |\n| `SignatureDoesNotMatch` | 403 | The request signature we calculated does not match the signature you provided. | Secret key is incorrect or signature is invalid |\n| `MissingAuthenticationToken` | 403 | Missing authentication token. | Authorization header is completely missing |\n| `InvalidCredential` | 403 | Invalid credential format. | Credential string has insufficient parts (expected 5 parts) |\n\n### Malformed Authorization Headers\n\n| Error Code | HTTP Status | Message | When Returned |\n|------------|-------------|---------|---------------|\n| `IncompleteSignature` | 403 | The authorization header is malformed. | Required components missing (Credential, SignedHeaders, or Signature) |\n| `InvalidAlgorithm` | 403 | The authorization header algorithm is not supported. | Algorithm is not AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 |\n| `AccessDenied` | 403 | Access Denied. | Generic authentication failure |\n\n### Timing Issues\n\n| Error Code | HTTP Status | Message | When Returned |\n|------------|-------------|---------|---------------|\n| `RequestExpired` | 403 | Request has expired. | Request timestamp is outside the allowed window (±15 minutes) |\n| `MissingDateHeader` | 403 | Missing required header: x-amz-date. | X-Amz-Date header is missing |\n\n## Malformed Signature Scenarios\n\nARMOR validates signature format and provides specific error codes:\n\n| Scenario | Error Code | Performance |\n|----------|------------|-------------|\n| Non-hex signature characters | `SignatureDoesNotMatch` | < 50ms |\n| Too short signature (< 32 bytes) | `SignatureDoesNotMatch` | < 50ms |\n| Empty signature | `IncompleteSignature` | < 50ms |\n| Random characters in signature | `SignatureDoesNotMatch` | < 50ms |\n\n## Performance Guarantees\n\nAll authentication rejection scenarios respond within strict time limits:\n\n| Test Type | Target | Actual | Environment |\n|-----------|--------|--------|-------------|\n| Unit test rejections | < 100ms | < 1ms | Local httptest |\n| Integration test rejections | < 500ms | < 50ms | Real server |\n| Malformed signature rejections | < 50ms | < 1ms | Local httptest |\n\n### Performance Test Coverage\n\nThe test suite includes performance verification for all rejection scenarios:\n\n1. **TestInvalidCredentialRejection/Rejection_happens_quickly** - Verifies < 100ms response time\n2. **TestMalformedSignatureRejection/Rejection_happens_quickly** - Verifies < 50ms response time for malformed signatures\n\n## Error Message Quality\n\nAll error responses include:\n\n1. **Specific Error Code** - Identifies the exact problem (e.g., `InvalidAccessKeyId`, `SignatureDoesNotMatch`)\n2. **Meaningful Message** - Human-readable description of the problem\n3. **XML Format** - S3-compatible XML structure with proper escaping\n\n### Message Validation\n\nTest suite verifies that:\n- Error messages are never empty\n- Messages are at least 10 characters long\n- Messages contain relevant keywords (authentication, signature, credential, algorithm, header, aws4)\n- XML is properly escaped to prevent injection\n\n## Response Consistency\n\nAll error responses maintain consistency:\n\n### Headers\n- Always return `Content-Type: application/xml`\n- Always return appropriate HTTP status code (403 for auth errors)\n- Response body is never empty\n\n### Structure\n- XML declaration with encoding: `<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`\n- Root element: `<Error>`\n- Two child elements: `<Code>` and `<Message>`\n- Proper XML escaping for special characters\n\n## Test Coverage Summary\n\nThe ARMOR test suite includes comprehensive coverage for rejection scenarios:\n\n### Unit Tests (`invalid_credential_test.go`)\n- 12 test scenarios covering:\n  - Invalid AWS credentials\n  - Malformed signatures  \n  - Missing authentication headers\n  - Malformed authorization headers\n  - Insufficient credential parts\n  - Missing required components (SignedHeaders, Signature, date)\n  - Expired requests\n  - Performance validation\n\n### Unit Tests (`malformed_signature_test.go`)\n- 20+ test scenarios covering:\n  - Garbage signature strings (non-hex, too short, empty, random chars)\n  - Invalid signature formats (missing algorithm, wrong algorithm, missing components)\n  - Partial signatures (missing components)\n  - Error message quality\n  - Performance validation\n\n### Integration Tests (`invalid_credential_integration_test.go`)\n- Real server tests with actual HTTP client\n- Performance validation under realistic conditions\n- End-to-end verification of error responses\n\n### Headers Consistency (`error_response_test.go`)\n- Verifies consistent headers across all rejection types\n- Validates Content-Type header\n- Ensures proper XML structure\n\n## Examples\n\n### Example 1: Invalid Access Key\n\n**Request:**\n```http\nGET /test-bucket/test-key HTTP/1.1\nHost: test-bucket.s3.us-east-005.backblazeb2.com\nAuthorization: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=INVALIDKEY/20250714/us-east-005/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=abc123...\nX-Amz-Date: 20250714T044805Z\n```\n\n**Response:**\n```http\nHTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden\nContent-Type: application/xml\n\n<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>InvalidAccessKeyId</Code>\n  <Message>The AWS Access Key Id you provided does not exist in our records.</Message>\n</Error>\n```\n\n### Example 2: Missing Authentication\n\n**Request:**\n```http\nGET /test-bucket/test-key HTTP/1.1\nHost: test-bucket.s3.us-east-005.backblazeb2.com\n```\n\n**Response:**\n```http\nHTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden\nContent-Type: application/xml\n\n<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>MissingAuthenticationToken</Code>\n  <Message>Missing authentication token.</Message>\n</Error>\n```\n\n### Example 3: Malformed Signature\n\n**Request:**\n```http\nGET /test-bucket/test-key HTTP/1.1\nHost: test-bucket.s3.us-east-005.backblazeb2.com\nAuthorization: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20250714/us-east-005/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host, Signature=!@#$%^&*()\nX-Amz-Date: 20250714T044805Z\n```\n\n**Response:**\n```http\nHTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden\nContent-Type: application/xml\n\n<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>SignatureDoesNotMatch</Code>\n  <Message>The request signature we calculated does not match the signature you provided.</Message>\n</Error>\n```\n\n## Implementation\n\nError responses are generated by two implementations:\n\n1. **Server Handler** (`internal/server/server.go:796-805`)\n   ```go\n   func (s *Server) writeError(w http.ResponseWriter, code, message string, statusCode int) {\n       w.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n       w.WriteHeader(statusCode)\n       // XML generation with proper escaping\n   }\n   ```\n\n2. **Handlers Package** (`internal/server/handlers/handlers.go:2695-2704`)\n   ```go\n   func (h *Handlers) writeError(w http.ResponseWriter, code, message string, statusCode int) {\n       w.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n       w.WriteHeader(statusCode)\n       // XML generation with proper escaping\n   }\n   ```\n\nBoth implementations ensure:\n- Consistent Content-Type header\n- Proper XML escaping to prevent injection\n- S3-compatible format\n\n## Testing\n\nTo run the error response test suite:\n\n```bash\n# Run all rejection tests\ngo test -v -run \"TestInvalidCredentialRejection|TestMalformedSignatureRejection\" ./internal/server/\n\n# Run headers consistency test\ngo test -v -run TestErrorResponseHeadersConsistency ./internal/server/\n\n# Run integration tests (requires INTEGRATION_TEST=1)\nINTEGRATION_TEST=1 go test -v -run TestInvalidCredentialsIntegration ./internal/server/\n```\n\n## Maintenance\n\nWhen adding new error scenarios:\n\n1. **Add test coverage** - Create tests in appropriate test file\n2. **Verify error code** - Use existing S3 error code when possible\n3. **Check performance** - Ensure response time < 100ms\n4. **Validate headers** - Confirm Content-Type and XML structure\n5. **Update this doc** - Document new error code and scenario\n\n## References\n\n- [S3 Error Responses](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/API/ErrorResponses.html)\n- Test files:\n  - `internal/server/invalid_credential_test.go`\n  - `internal/server/malformed_signature_test.go`\n  - `internal/server/invalid_credential_integration_test.go`\n  - `internal/server/error_response_test.go`\n","numLines":272,"startLine":1,"totalLines":272}}}

It serves as a quick reference for understanding ARMOR's S3 API compatibility.\n10\t\n11\t**Related Documentation:**\n12\t- [Error Response Header Specification](./error-header-spec.md) - Comprehensive error response documentation\n13\t- [Error Responses](./error-responses.md) - Detailed error response documentation\n14\t\n15\t## Response Header Categories\n16\t\n17\t### 1. Standard HTTP Headers (All Responses)\n18\t\n19\t| Header | Presence | Description |\n20\t|--------|----------|-------------|\n21\t| `Content-Type` | All responses | `application/xml` for errors; varies for success (object content type) |\n22\t| `Content-Length` | Success responses | Size of response body (not set for error responses) |\n23\t| `Content-Range` | Partial GET | Range response format: `bytes {start}-{end}/{total}` |\n24\t| `Content-Disposition` | Conditional | Set when object has `Content-Disposition` metadata |\n25\t| `Accept-Ranges` | HEAD/GET | Always `bytes` (indicates range request support) |\n26\t\n27\t### 2. S3-Specific Headers (Success Responses)\n28\t\n29\t| Header | Format | When Returned | Example |\n30\t|--------|--------|---------------|---------|\n31\t| `ETag` | `\"{md5hash}\"` | PUT success, GET/HEAD success, ListObjects entries | `\"5d41402abc4b2a76b9719d911017c592\"` |\n32\t| `Last-Modified` | HTTP TimeFormat | GET/HEAD object, ListObjects entries | `Mon, 14 Jul 2026 05:00:00 GMT` |\n33\t\n34\t### 3. CORS Headers (HTTP 403 Only)\n35\t\n36\t| Header | Value | When Returned |\n37\t|--------|-------|---------------|\n38\t| `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` | `*` | HTTP 403 authentication/authorization errors only |\n39\t| `Access-Control-Allow-Methods` | `GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS` | HTTP 403 authentication/authorization errors only |\n40\t| `Access-Control-Allow-Headers` | `Authorization, Content-Type, Range, Content-Length` | HTTP 403 authentication/authorization errors only |\n41\t\n42\t### 4. ARMOR-Specific Headers\n43\t\n44\t| Header | Value | When Returned |\n45\t|--------|-------|---------------|\n46\t| `X-Armor-Streaming` | `true` | PUT object with streaming encryption (>10MB objects) |\n47\t\n48\t## Error Response Headers by HTTP Status\n49\t\n50\t### HTTP 400 Bad Request\n51\t\n52\t**Headers:**\n53\t```\n54\tContent-Type: application/xml\n55\t```\n56\t\n57\t**Error Codes:**\n58\t- `InvalidRequest` - Unsupported operations, invalid parameters\n59\t- `InvalidRange` - Invalid Range header format\n60\t- `MalformedXML` - Failed to parse request XML\n61\t\n62\t**No CORS headers**\n63\t\n64\t### HTTP 403 Forbidden\n65\t\n66\t**Headers:**\n67\t```\n68\tContent-Type: application/xml\n69\tAccess-Control-Allow-Origin: *\n70\tAccess-Control-Allow-Methods: GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS\n71\tAccess-Control-Allow-Headers: Authorization, Content-Type, Range, Content-Length\n72\t```\n73\t\n74\t**Error Codes:**\n75\t- `MissingAuthenticationToken` - Authorization header missing\n76\t- `InvalidAccessKeyId` - Invalid access key\n77\t- `SignatureDoesNotMatch` - Signature validation failed\n78\t- `RequestExpired` - Request timestamp outside 15-minute window\n79\t- `InvalidAlgorithm` - Non-AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 algorithm\n80\t- `IncompleteSignature` - Authorization header missing fields\n81\t- `MissingDateHeader` - X-Amz-Date header missing\n82\t- `InvalidDateFormat` - Invalid date format\n83\t- `InvalidCredential` - Invalid credential format\n84\t- `AccessDenied` - ACL-based authorization rejection\n85\t\n86\t**CORS headers present** (only error type with CORS)\n87\t\n88\t### HTTP 404 Not Found\n89\t\n90\t**Headers:**\n91\t```\n92\tContent-Type: application/xml\n93\t```\n94\t\n95\t**Error Codes:**\n96\t- `NoSuchKey` - Object not found\n97\t- `NoSuchBucket` - Bucket not found\n98\t- `NoSuchUpload` - Multipart upload not found\n99\t\n100\t**No CORS headers**\n101\t\n102\t### HTTP 405 Method Not Allowed\n103\t\n104\t**Headers:**\n105\t```\n106\tContent-Type: application/xml\n107\t```\n108\t\n109\t**Error Codes:**\n110\t- `MethodNotAllowed` - HTTP method not supported\n111\t\n112\t**No CORS headers**\n113\t\n114\t### HTTP 412 Precondition Failed\n115\t\n116\t**Headers:**\n117\t```\n118\tContent-Type: application/xml\n119\t```\n120\t\n121\t**Error Codes:**\n122\t- `PreconditionFailed` - If-Match/If-Unmodified-Since condition failed\n123\t\n124\t**No CORS headers**\n125\t\n126\t### HTTP 500 Internal Server Error\n127\t\n128\t**Headers:**\n129\t```\n130\tContent-Type: application/xml\n131\t```\n132\t\n133\t**Error Codes:**\n134\t- `InternalError` - Backend failures, encryption/decryption errors\n135\t\n136\t**No CORS headers**\n137\t\n138\t### HTTP 503 Service Unavailable\n139\t\n140\t**Headers:**\n141\t```\n142\tContent-Type: application/xml\n143\t```\n144\t\n145\t**Error Codes:**\n146\t- `ServiceUnavailable` - Health check failures\n147\t\n148\t**No CORS headers**\n149\t\n150\t## Success Response Headers by Operation\n151\t\n152\t### GET Object (200)\n153\t\n154\t**Headers:**\n155\t```\n156\tContent-Type: {object-content-type}\n157\tContent-Length: {object-size}\n158\tETag: \"{etag-md5}\"\n159\tLast-Modified: {http-timeformat}\n160\tAccept-Ranges: bytes\n161\tContent-Disposition: {disposition}  (if present in metadata)\n162\tContent-Range: bytes {start}-{end}/{total}  (for Range requests)\n163\t```\n164\t\n165\t**Conditional Request Responses (304):**\n166\t- Returns `304 Not Modified` with no body when `If-Match`/`If-None-Match`/`If-Modified-Since`/`If-Unmodified-Since` conditions are met\n167\t\n168\t### HEAD Object (200)\n169\t\n170\t**Headers:**\n171\t```\n172\tContent-Type: {object-content-type}\n173\tContent-Length: {object-size}\n174\tETag: \"{etag-md5}\"\n175\tLast-Modified: {http-timeformat}\n176\tAccept-Ranges: bytes\n177\t```\n178\t\n179\t**No response body**\n180\t\n181\t### PUT Object (200)\n182\t\n183\t**Headers:**\n184\t```\n185\tETag: \"{etag-md5}\"\n186\tX-Armor-Streaming: true  (for >10MB objects using streaming encryption)\n187\t```\n188\t\n189\t**No response body**\n190\t\n191\t### DELETE Object (204)\n192\t\n193\t**Headers:**\n194\t```\n195\t(no headers)\n196\t```\n197\t\n198\t**No response body** (HTTP 204 No Content)\n199\t\n200\t### ListObjectsV2 (200)\n201\t\n202\t**Headers:**\n203\t```\n204\tContent-Type: application/xml\n205\t```\n206\t\n207\t**Response Body:** XML with `Contents` entries including `ETag` and `Last-Modified` for each object\n208\t\n209\t### CopyObject (200)\n210\t\n211\t**Headers:**\n212\t```\n213\tContent-Type: application/xml\n214\t```\n215\t\n216\t**Response Body:** XML with destination object's `ETag` and `Last-Modified`\n217\t\n218\t## Deviations from AWS S3\n219\t\n220\t### Missing Standard S3 Response Headers\n221\t\n222\tARMOR does **not** return the following standard AWS S3 response headers:\n223\t\n224\t| Missing Header | AWS Purpose | Impact |\n225\t|----------------|-------------|--------|\n226\t| `x-amz-request-id` | AWS request tracking ID | Medium - prevents request tracing in AWS-compatible tools |\n227\t| `x-amz-id-2` | AWS extended request ID (S3-specific) | Low - rarely used by clients |\n228\t\n229\t**Recommendation:** Consider adding `x-amz-request-id` header with a UUID for each request to improve AWS compatibility and request tracing.\n230\t\n231\t### Non-Standard Behaviors\n232\t\n233\t1. **ETag for Streaming Encryption:**\n234\t   - **AWS S3:** ETag is always MD5 hash of object content (multi-part objects have different ETag format)\n235\t   - **ARMOR:** For >10MB objects using streaming encryption, ETag is SHA-256 truncated to 16 bytes\n236\t   - **Impact:** Low - ETag format is opaque to most clients, but may affect conditional requests\n237\t   - **Mitigation:** ARMOR sets `X-Armor-Streaming: true` header to indicate this case\n238\t\n239\t2. **405 Method Not Allowed (Non-S3 Endpoints):**\n240\t   - **AWS S3:** Would return XML error response\n241\t   - **ARMOR:** Admin endpoints (e.g., `/admin/key/*`) return plain text \"Method not allowed\"\n242\t   - **Impact:** Low - admin endpoints are not S3-facing\n243\t   - **Status:** Documented in error-header-spec.md as Priority 2 remediation\n244\t\n245\t## Quick Reference Tables\n246\t\n247\t### Error Response Summary\n248\t\n249\t| HTTP Status | Content-Type | CORS | Error Codes |\n250\t|-------------|--------------|------|-------------|\n251\t| 400 | application/xml | No | InvalidRequest, InvalidRange, MalformedXML |\n252\t| 403 | application/xml | Yes | 10 auth error codes (MissingAuthenticationToken, etc.) |\n253\t| 404 | application/xml | No | NoSuchKey, NoSuchBucket, NoSuchUpload |\n254\t| 405 | application/xml | No | MethodNotAllowed |\n255\t| 412 | application/xml | No | PreconditionFailed |\n256\t| 500 | application/xml | No | InternalError |\n257\t| 503 | application/xml | No | ServiceUnavailable |\n258\t\n259\t### Success Response Header Summary\n260\t\n261\t| Operation | ETag | Last-Modified | Accept-Ranges | Other |\n262\t|-----------|------|---------------|----------------|-------|\n263\t| GET Object | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | Content-Type, Content-Length, Content-Range (if Range) |\n264\t| HEAD Object | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | Content-Type, Content-Length |\n265\t| PUT Object | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | X-Armor-Streaming (if >10MB) |\n266\t| DELETE Object | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | (no headers, 204 response) |\n267\t| ListObjectsV2 | ✓ (in body) | ✓ (in body) | ✗ | Content-Type: application/xml |\n268\t\n269\t## Implementation Files\n270\t\n271\t- **Error Responses:** `internal/server/handlers/handlers.go:2695-2704` (`writeError`)\n272\t- **Success Responses:** \n273\t  - GET Object: `internal/server/handlers/handlers.go:602` (`GetObject`)\n274\t  - HEAD Object: `internal/server/handlers/handlers.go:1133` (`HeadObject`)\n275\t  - PUT Object: `internal/server/handlers/handlers.go:267` (`PutObject`)\n276\t  - DELETE Object: `internal/server/handlers/handlers.go:1210` (`DeleteObject`)\n277\t  - ListObjectsV2: `internal/server/handlers/handlers.go:1470`\n278\t  - CopyObject: `internal/server/handlers/handlers.go:1238`\n279\t  - CORS: `internal/server/server.go:656-660`\n280\t\n281\t## Testing\n282\t\n283\tSee [Error Response Header Specification](./error-header-spec.md#testing-requirements) for comprehensive testing documentation.\n284\t\n285\t## References\n286\t\n287\t### AWS S3 Documentation\n288\t- [S3 Response ARMOR S3 Endpoint Response Headers\n\n**Version:** 1.0  \n**Date:** 2026-07-14  \n**Status:** Active\n\n## Overview\n\nThis document provides a focused inventory of all response headers returned by ARMOR's S3-facing endpoints, including both success and error responses. It serves as a quick reference for understanding ARMOR's S3 API compatibility.\n\n**Related Documentation:**\n- [Error Response Header Specification](./error-header-spec.md) - Comprehensive error response documentation\n- [Error Responses](./error-responses.md) - Detailed error response documentation\n\n## Response Header Categories\n\n### 1. Standard HTTP Headers (All Responses)\n\n| Header | Presence | Description |\n|--------|----------|-------------|\n| `Content-Type` | All responses | `application/xml` for errors; varies for success (object content type) |\n| `Content-Length` | Success responses | Size of response body (not set for error responses) |\n| `Content-Range` | Partial GET | Range response format: `bytes {start}-{end}/{total}` |\n| `Content-Disposition` | Conditional | Set when object has `Content-Disposition` metadata |\n| `Accept-Ranges` | HEAD/GET | Always `bytes` (indicates range request support) |\n\n### 2. S3-Specific Headers (Success Responses)\n\n| Header | Format | When Returned | Example |\n|--------|--------|---------------|---------|\n| `ETag` | `\"{md5hash}\"` | PUT success, GET/HEAD success, ListObjects entries | `\"5d41402abc4b2a76b9719d911017c592\"` |\n| `Last-Modified` | HTTP TimeFormat | GET/HEAD object, ListObjects entries | `Mon, 14 Jul 2026 05:00:00 GMT` |\n\n### 3. CORS Headers (HTTP 403 Only)\n\n| Header | Value | When Returned |\n|--------|-------|---------------|\n| `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` | `*` | HTTP 403 authentication/authorization errors only |\n| `Access-Control-Allow-Methods` | `GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS` | HTTP 403 authentication/authorization errors only |\n| `Access-Control-Allow-Headers` | `Authorization, Content-Type, Range, Content-Length` | HTTP 403 authentication/authorization errors only |\n\n### 4. ARMOR-Specific Headers\n\n| Header | Value | When Returned |\n|--------|-------|---------------|\n| `X-Armor-Streaming` | `true` | PUT object with streaming encryption (>10MB objects) |\n\n## Error Response Headers by HTTP Status\n\n### HTTP 400 Bad Request\n\n**Headers:**\n```\nContent-Type: application/xml\n```\n\n**Error Codes:**\n- `InvalidRequest` - Unsupported operations, invalid parameters\n- `InvalidRange` - Invalid Range header format\n- `MalformedXML` - Failed to parse request XML\n\n**No CORS headers**\n\n### HTTP 403 Forbidden\n\n**Headers:**\n```\nContent-Type: application/xml\nAccess-Control-Allow-Origin: *\nAccess-Control-Allow-Methods: GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS\nAccess-Control-Allow-Headers: Authorization, Content-Type, Range, Content-Length\n```\n\n**Error Codes:**\n- `MissingAuthenticationToken` - Authorization header missing\n- `InvalidAccessKeyId` - Invalid access key\n- `SignatureDoesNotMatch` - Signature validation failed\n- `RequestExpired` - Request timestamp outside 15-minute window\n- `InvalidAlgorithm` - Non-AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 algorithm\n- `IncompleteSignature` - Authorization header missing fields\n- `MissingDateHeader` - X-Amz-Date header missing\n- `InvalidDateFormat` - Invalid date format\n- `InvalidCredential` - Invalid credential format\n- `AccessDenied` - ACL-based authorization rejection\n\n**CORS headers present** (only error type with CORS)\n\n### HTTP 404 Not Found\n\n**Headers:**\n```\nContent-Type: application/xml\n```\n\n**Error Codes:**\n- `NoSuchKey` - Object not found\n- `NoSuchBucket` - Bucket not found\n- `NoSuchUpload` - Multipart upload not found\n\n**No CORS headers**\n\n### HTTP 405 Method Not Allowed\n\n**Headers:**\n```\nContent-Type: application/xml\n```\n\n**Error Codes:**\n- `MethodNotAllowed` - HTTP method not supported\n\n**No CORS headers**\n\n### HTTP 412 Precondition Failed\n\n**Headers:**\n```\nContent-Type: application/xml\n```\n\n**Error Codes:**\n- `PreconditionFailed` - If-Match/If-Unmodified-Since condition failed\n\n**No CORS headers**\n\n### HTTP 500 Internal Server Error\n\n**Headers:**\n```\nContent-Type: application/xml\n```\n\n**Error Codes:**\n- `InternalError` - Backend failures, encryption/decryption errors\n\n**No CORS headers**\n\n### HTTP 503 Service Unavailable\n\n**Headers:**\n```\nContent-Type: application/xml\n```\n\n**Error Codes:**\n- `ServiceUnavailable` - Health check failures\n\n**No CORS headers**\n\n## Success Response Headers by Operation\n\n### GET Object (200)\n\n**Headers:**\n```\nContent-Type: {object-content-type}\nContent-Length: {object-size}\nETag: \"{etag-md5}\"\nLast-Modified: {http-timeformat}\nAccept-Ranges: bytes\nContent-Disposition: {disposition}  (if present in metadata)\nContent-Range: bytes {start}-{end}/{total}  (for Range requests)\n```\n\n**Conditional Request Responses (304):**\n- Returns `304 Not Modified` with no body when `If-Match`/`If-None-Match`/`If-Modified-Since`/`If-Unmodified-Since` conditions are met\n\n### HEAD Object (200)\n\n**Headers:**\n```\nContent-Type: {object-content-type}\nContent-Length: {object-size}\nETag: \"{etag-md5}\"\nLast-Modified: {http-timeformat}\nAccept-Ranges: bytes\n```\n\n**No response body**\n\n### PUT Object (200)\n\n**Headers:**\n```\nETag: \"{etag-md5}\"\nX-Armor-Streaming: true  (for >10MB objects using streaming encryption)\n```\n\n**No response body**\n\n### DELETE Object (204)\n\n**Headers:**\n```\n(no headers)\n```\n\n**No response body** (HTTP 204 No Content)\n\n### ListObjectsV2 (200)\n\n**Headers:**\n```\nContent-Type: application/xml\n```\n\n**Response Body:** XML with `Contents` entries including `ETag` and `Last-Modified` for each object\n\n### CopyObject (200)\n\n**Headers:**\n```\nContent-Type: application/xml\n```\n\n**Response Body:** XML with destination object's `ETag` and `Last-Modified`\n\n## Deviations from AWS S3\n\n### Missing Standard S3 Response Headers\n\nARMOR does **not** return the following standard AWS S3 response headers:\n\n| Missing Header | AWS Purpose | Impact |\n|----------------|-------------|--------|\n| `x-amz-request-id` | AWS request tracking ID | Medium - prevents request tracing in AWS-compatible tools |\n| `x-amz-id-2` | AWS extended request ID (S3-specific) | Low - rarely used by clients |\n\n**Recommendation:** Consider adding `x-amz-request-id` header with a UUID for each request to improve AWS compatibility and request tracing.\n\n### Non-Standard Behaviors\n\n1. **ETag for Streaming Encryption:**\n   - **AWS S3:** ETag is always MD5 hash of object content (multi-part objects have different ETag format)\n   - **ARMOR:** For >10MB objects using streaming encryption, ETag is SHA-256 truncated to 16 bytes\n   - **Impact:** Low - ETag format is opaque to most clients, but may affect conditional requests\n   - **Mitigation:** ARMOR sets `X-Armor-Streaming: true` header to indicate this case\n\n2. **405 Method Not Allowed (Non-S3 Endpoints):**\n   - **AWS S3:** Would return XML error response\n   - **ARMOR:** Admin endpoints (e.g., `/admin/key/*`) return plain text \"Method not allowed\"\n   - **Impact:** Low - admin endpoints are not S3-facing\n   - **Status:** Documented in error-header-spec.md as Priority 2 remediation\n\n## Quick Reference Tables\n\n### Error Response Summary\n\n| HTTP Status | Content-Type | CORS | Error Codes |\n|-------------|--------------|------|-------------|\n| 400 | application/xml | No | InvalidRequest, InvalidRange, MalformedXML |\n| 403 | application/xml | Yes | 10 auth error codes (MissingAuthenticationToken, etc.) |\n| 404 | application/xml | No | NoSuchKey, NoSuchBucket, NoSuchUpload |\n| 405 | application/xml | No | MethodNotAllowed |\n| 412 | application/xml | No | PreconditionFailed |\n| 500 | application/xml | No | InternalError |\n| 503 | application/xml | No | ServiceUnavailable |\n\n### Success Response Header Summary\n\n| Operation | ETag | Last-Modified | Accept-Ranges | Other |\n|-----------|------|---------------|----------------|-------|\n| GET Object | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | Content-Type, Content-Length, Content-Range (if Range) |\n| HEAD Object | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | Content-Type, Content-Length |\n| PUT Object | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | X-Armor-Streaming (if >10MB) |\n| DELETE Object | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | (no headers, 204 response) |\n| ListObjectsV2 | ✓ (in body) | ✓ (in body) | ✗ | Content-Type: application/xml |\n\n## Implementation Files\n\n- **Error Responses:** `internal/server/handlers/handlers.go:2695-2704` (`writeError`)\n- **Success Responses:** \n  - GET Object: `internal/server/handlers/handlers.go:602` (`GetObject`)\n  - HEAD Object: `internal/server/handlers/handlers.go:1133` (`HeadObject`)\n  - PUT Object: `internal/server/handlers/handlers.go:267` (`PutObject`)\n  - DELETE Object: `internal/server/handlers/handlers.go:1210` (`DeleteObject`)\n  - ListObjectsV2: `internal/server/handlers/handlers.go:1470`\n  - CopyObject: `internal/server/handlers/handlers.go:1238`\n  - CORS: `internal/server/server.go:656-660`\n\n## Testing\n\nSee [Error Response Header Specification](./error-header-spec.md#testing-requirements) for comprehensive testing documentation.\n\n## References\n\n### AWS S3 Documentation\n- [S3 Response Headers](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/API/RESTCommonResponseHeaders.html)\n- [S3 Error Responses](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/API/ErrorResponses.html)\n\n### Internal Documentation\n- [Error Response Header Specification](./error-header-spec.md)\n- [Error Responses](./error-responses.md)\n- [Authentication Rejection Headers](./auth-rejection-headers.md)\n\n---\n\n**End of Document**\n","numLines":299,"startLine":1,"totalLines":299}}}

Health Check Endpoint\n33\t\n34\t#### `/healthz` - Liveness Probe\n35\t\n36\t**Method:** GET\n37\t\n38\t**Success Response (200 OK):**\n39\t```\n40\tStatus: 200 OK\n41\tContent-Type: text/plain\n42\tContent-Length: 2\n43\t\n44\tOK\n45\t```\n46\t\n47\t**Headers:**\n48\t- No special headers\n49\t- Returns plain text \"OK\"\n50\t\n51\t**Error Responses:** None (always returns 200)\n52\t\n53\t---\n54\t\n55\t### 2. Key Management Endpoints\n56\t\n57\t#### `/admin/key/verify` - Verify MEK\n58\t\n59\t**Method:** GET\n60\t\n61\t**Success Response (200 OK):**\n62\t```json\n63\tStatus: 200 OK\n64\tContent-Type: application/json\n65\t\n66\t{\n67\t  \"status\": \"verified\",\n68\t  \"message\": \"MEK is correct\"\n69\t}\n70\t```\n71\t\n72\t**Alternative Success (200 OK - when canary not configured):**\n73\t```json\n74\tStatus: 200 OK\n75\tContent-Type: application/json\n76\t\n77\t{\n78\t  \"status\": \"unknown\",\n79\t  \"error\": \"canary monitor not configured\"\n80\t}\n81\t```\n82\t\n83\t**Error Response (503 Service Unavailable):**\n84\t```json\n85\tStatus: 503 Service Unavailable\n86\tContent-Type: application/json\n87\t\n88\t{\n89\t  \"status\": \"unverified\",\n90\t  \"error\": \"canary check failed - MEK may be incorrect\"\n91\t}\n92\t```\n93\t\n94\t**Error Response (405 Method Not Allowed):**\n95\t```\n96\tStatus: 405 Method Not Allowed\n97\tContent-Type: text/plain\n98\t\n99\tMethod not allowed\n100\t```\n101\t\n102\t**Headers:**\n103\t- Success: `Content-Type: application/json`\n104\t- Error (405): `Content-Type: text/plain` ⚠️ **INCONSISTENT**\n105\t\n106\t**Code Reference:** `/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/server/server.go:486-507`\n107\t\n108\t---\n109\t\n110\t#### `/admin/key/rotate` - Rotate Master Encryption Key\n111\t\n112\t**Method:** POST\n113\t\n114\t**Success Response (200 OK):**\n115\t```json\n116\tStatus: 200 OK\n117\tContent-Type: application/json\n118\t\n119\t{\n120\t  \"status\": \"completed\",\n121\t  \"rotated_objects\": 123,\n122\t  \"failed_objects\": 0,\n123\t  \"duration_ms\": 45678,\n124\t  \"started_at\": \"2024-01-01T00:00:00Z\",\n125\t  \"completed_at\": \"2024-01-01T00:00:45Z\"\n126\t}\n127\t```\n128\t\n129\t**Error Response (400 Bad Request - Body Read Error):**\n130\t```\n131\tStatus: 400 Bad Request\n132\tContent-Type: text/plain\n133\t\n134\tFailed to read request body: <error details>\n135\t```\n136\t\n137\t**Error Response (400 Bad Request - Invalid Hex):**\n138\t```\n139\tStatus: 400 Bad Request\n140\tContent-Type: text/plain\n141\t\n142\tInvalid hex-encoded MEK\n143\t```\n144\t\n145\t**Error Response (400 Bad Request - Invalid Length):**\n146\t```\n147\tStatus: 400 Bad Request\n148\tContent-Type: text/plain\n149\t\n150\tInvalid MEK length: expected 32 bytes or 64 hex chars, got <actual length>\n151\t```\n152\t\n153\t**Error Response (405 Method Not Allowed):**\n154\t```\n155\tStatus: 405 Method Not Allowed\n156\tContent-Type: text/plain\n157\t\n158\tMethod not allowed\n159\t```\n160\t\n161\t**Error Response (500 Internal Server Error):**\n162\t```json\n163\tStatus: 500 Internal Server Error\n164\tContent-Type: application/json\n165\t\n166\t{\n167\t  \"status\": \"failed\",\n168\t  \"error\": \"<error message>\",\n169\t  \"result\": {\n170\t    \"rotated_objects\": 10,\n171\t    \"failed_objects\": 1,\n172\t    ...\n173\t  }\n174\t}\n175\t```\n176\t\n177\t**Headers:**\n178\t- Success: `Content-Type: application/json`\n179\t- Errors (400/405): `Content-Type: text/plain` ⚠️ **INCONSISTENT**\n180\t- Server Error (500): `Content-Type: application/json`\n181\t\n182\t**Code Reference:** `/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/server/server.go:510-573`\n183\t\n184\t---\n185\t\n186\t#### `/admin/key/export` - Export Current MEK\n187\t\n188\t**Method:** GET\n189\t\n190\t**Error Response (400 Bad Request - Missing Confirmation):**\n191\t```\n192\tStatus: 400 Bad Request\n193\tContent-Type: text/plain\n194\t\n195\tMust include ?confirm=yes to export key\n196\t```\n197\t\n198\t**Error Response (405 Method Not Allowed):**\n199\t```\n200\tStatus: 405 Method Not Allowed\n201\tContent-Type: text/plain\n202\t\n203\tMethod not allowed\n204\t```\n205\t\n206\t**Success Response (200 OK):**\n207\t```json\n208\tStatus: 200 OK\n209\tContent-Type: application/json\n210\t\n211\t{\n212\t  \"mek\": \"64-char-hex-encoded-key\",\n213\t  \"format\": \"hex\",\n214\t  \"warning\": \"This key provides access to all encrypted data. Store securely.\"\n215\t}\n216\t```\n217\t\n218\t**Headers:**\n219\t- Success: `Content-Type: application/json`\n220\t- Errors: `Content-Type: text/plain` ⚠️ **INCONSISTENT**\n221\t\n222\t**Code Reference:** `/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/server/server.go:576-596`\n223\t\n224\t---\n225\t\n226\t### 3. Pre-signed URL Endpoint\n227\t\n228\t#### `/admin/presign` - Generate Pre-signed URL\n229\t\n230\t**Method:** POST\n231\t\n232\t**Request Body:**\n233\t```json\n234\t{\n235\t  \"bucket\": \"my-bucket\",           // Optional, defaults to configured bucket\n236\t  \"key\": \"path/to/file.parquet\",   // Required\n237\t  \"expires_in\": \"1h\",              // Optional, defaults to 1h\n238\t  \"content_disposition\": \"...\",    // Optional\n239\t  \"range\": \"bytes=0-1023\"          // Optional\n240\t}\n241\t```\n242\t\n243\t**Success Response (200 OK):**\n244\t```json\n245\tStatus: 200 OK\n246\tContent-Type: application/json\n247\t\n248\t{\n249\t  \"url\": \"https://...\",\n250\t  \"expires_in\": \"1h\",\n251\t  \"expires_at\": \"2024-01-01T01:00:00Z\"\n252\t}\n253\t```\n254\t\n255\t**Error Response (405 Method Not Allowed):**\n256\t```\n257\tStatus: 405 Method Not Allowed\n258\tContent-Type: text/plain\n259\t\n260\tMethod not allowed\n261\t```\n262\t\n263\t**Error Response (403 Forbidden - Auth Error):**\n264\t```xml\n265\tStatus: 403 Forbidden\n266\tContent-Type: application/xml\n267\t\n268\t<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n269\t<Error>\n270\t  <Code>AccessDenied</Code>\n271\t  <Message>Invalid credentials</Message>\n272\t</Error>\n273\t```\n274\t\n275\t**Error Response (403 Forbidden - ACL Denied):**\n276\t```xml\n277\tStatus: 403 Forbidden\n278\tContent-Type: application/xml\n279\t\n280\t<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n281\t<Error>\n282\t  <Code>AccessDenied</Code>\n283\t  <Message>Access Denied</Message>\n284\t</Error>\n285\t```\n286\t\n287\t**Error Response (400 Bad Request - Invalid Body):**\n288\t```\n289\tStatus: 400 Bad Request\n290\tContent-Type: text/plain\n291\t\n292\tInvalid request body: <error details>\n293\t```\n294\t\n295\t**Error Response (400 Bad Request - Missing Key):**\n296\t```\n297\tStatus: 400 Bad Request\n298\tContent-Type: text/plain\n299\t\n300\tkey is required\n301\t```\n302\t\n303\t**Error Response (400 Bad Request - Invalid Expiration):**\n304\t```\n305\tStatus: 400 Bad Request\n306\tContent-Type: text/plain\n307\t\n308\tInvalid expires_in: <error details>\n309\t```\n310\t\n311\t**Error Response (500 Internal Server Error):**\n312\t```\n313\tStatus: 500 Internal Server Error\n314\tContent-Type: text/plain\n315\t\n316\tFailed to generate URL: <error details>\n317\t```\n318\t\n319\t**Headers:**\n320\t- Success: `Content-Type: application/json`\n321\t- Auth Errors (403): `Content-Type: application/xml` ⚠️ **INCONSISTENT**\n322\t- Other Errors: `Content-Type: text/plain` ⚠️ **INCONSISTENT**\n323\t\n324\t**Code Reference:** `/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/server/server.go:828-913`\n325\t\n326\t---\n327\t\n328\t### 4. B2 Key Management Endpoints\n329\t\n330\t#### `/admin/b2/keys` - List/Create B2 Keys\n331\t\n332\t**Methods:** GET (list), POST (create)\n333\t\n334\t**GET - List Keys:**\n335\t\n336\t**Success Response (200 OK):**\n337\t```json\n338\tStatus: 200 OK\n339\tContent-Type: application/json\n340\t\n341\t{\n342\t  \"keys\": [\n343\t    {\n344\t      \"id\": \"keyId1\",\n345\t      \"name\": \"key-name\",\n346\t      \"capabilities\": [\"readFiles\", \"writeFiles\"],\n347\t      \"key_id\": \"appId_keyId\",\n348\t      \"secret_key\": \"appKey...\",\n349\t      \"expires_at\": \"2024-01-01T01:00:00Z\"\n350\t    }\n351\t  ],\n352\t  \"next_cursor\": \"cursor-for-next-page\"\n353\t}\n354\t```\n355\t\n356\t**Error Response (503 Service Unavailable):**\n357\t```\n358\tStatus: 503 Service Unavailable\n359\tContent-Type: text/plain\n360\t\n361\t{\"error\":\"B2 key management not available - check B2 credentials\"}\n362\t```\n363\t\n364\t**Error Response (500 Internal Server Error):**\n365\t```\n366\tStatus: 500 Internal Server Error\n367\tContent-Type: text/plain\n368\t\n369\t{\"error\":\"Failed to list keys: <error details>\"}\n370\t```\n371\t\n372\t**POST - Create Key:**\n373\t\n374\t**Request Body:**\n375\t```json\n376\t{\n377\t  \"name\": \"key-name\",\n378\t  \"capabilities\": [\"readFiles\", \"writeFiles\"],\n379\t  \"valid_duration_seconds\": 3600\n380\t}\n381\t```\n382\t\n383\t**Success Response (201 Created):**\n384\t```json\n385\tStatus: 201 Created\n386\tContent-Type: application/json\n387\t\n388\t{\n389\t  \"id\": \"keyId1\",\n390\t  \"name\": \"key-name\",\n391\t  \"capabilities\": [\"readFiles\", \"writeFiles\"],\n392\t  \"key_id\": \"appId_keyId\",\n393\t  \"secret_key\": \"appKey...\",\n394\t  \"expires_at\": \"2024-01-01T01:00:00Z\"\n395\t}\n396\t```\n397\t\n398\t**Error Response (503 Service Unavailable):**\n399\t```\n400\tStatus: 503 Service Unavailable\n401\tContent-Type: text/plain\n402\t\n403\t{\"error\":\"B2 key management not available - check B2 credentials\"}\n404\t```\n405\t\n406\t**Error Response (400 Bad Request - Invalid Body):**\n407\t```\n408\tStatus: 400 Bad Request\n409\tContent-Type: text/plain\n410\t\n411\t{\"error\":\"Invalid request body: <error details>\"}\n412\t```\n413\t\n414\t**Error Response (400 Bad Request - Missing Name):**\n415\t```\n416\tStatus: 400 Bad Request\n417\tContent-Type: text/plain\n418\t\n419\t{\"error\":\"name is required\"}\n420\t```\n421\t\n422\t**Error Response (400 Bad Request - Missing Capabilities):**\n423\t```\n424\tStatus: 400 Bad Request\n425\tContent-Type: text/plain\n426\t\n427\t{\"error\":\"capabilities is required\"}\n428\t```\n429\t\n430\t**Error Response (405 Method Not Allowed):**\n431\t```\n432\tStatus: 405 Method Not Allowed\n433\tContent-Type: text/plain\n434\t\n435\tMethod not allowed\n436\t```\n437\t\n438\t**Error Response (500 Internal Server Error):**\n439\t```\n440\tStatus: 500 Internal Server Error\n441\tContent-Type: text/plain\n442\t\n443\t{\"error\":\"Failed to create key: <error details>\"}\n444\t```\n445\t\n446\t**Headers:**\n447\t- Success: `Content-Type: application/json`\n448\t- Errors: `Content-Type: text/plain` (but JSON body) ⚠️ **INCONSISTENT**\n449\t- Note: Error responses claim to be plain text but contain JSON\n450\t\n451\t**Code Reference:** `/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/server/server.go:1246-1324`\n452\t\n453\t---\n454\t\n455\t#### `/admin/b2/keys/{id}` - Delete B2 Key\n456\t\n457\t**Method:** DELETE\n458\t\n459\t**Success Response (204 No Content):**\n460\t```\n461\tStatus: 204 No Content\n462\t```\n463\t\n464\t**Error Response (503 Service Unavailable):**\n465\t```\n466\tStatus: 503 Service Unavailable\n467\tContent-Type: text/plain\n468\t\n469\t{\"error\":\"B2 key management not available - check B2 credentials\"}\n470\t```\n471\t\n472\t**Error Response (405 Method Not Allowed):**\n473\t```\n474\tStatus: 405 Method Not Allowed\n475\tContent-Type: text/plain\n476\t\n477\tMethod not allowed\n478\t```\n479\t\n480\t**Error Response (400 Bad Request - Missing Key ID):**\n481\t```\n482\tStatus: 400 Bad Request\n483\tContent-Type: text/plain\n484\t\n485\t{\"error\":\"key ID is required\"}\n486\t```\n487\t\n488\t**Error Response (404 Not Found):**\n489\t```\n490\tStatus: 404 Not Found\n491\tContent-Type: text/plain\n492\t\n493\t{\"error\":\"key not found\"}\n494\t```\n495\t\n496\t**Error Response (500 Internal Server Error):**\n497\t```\n498\tStatus: 500 Internal Server Error\n499\tContent-Type: text/plain\n500\t\n501\t{\"error\":\"Failed to delete key: <error details>\"}\n502\t```\n503\t\n504\t**Headers:**\n505\t- Success: No headers (204)\n506\t- Errors: `Content-Type: text/plain` (but JSON body) ⚠️ **INCONSISTENT**\n507\t- Note: Error responses claim to be plain text but contain JSON\n508\t\n509\t**Code Reference:** `/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/server/server.go:1326-1364`\n510\t\n511\t---\n512\t\n513\t### 5. ARMOR Status Endpoints\n514\t\n515\t#### `/armor/canary` - Canary Status\n516\t\n517\t**Method:** GET\n518\t\n519\t**Success Response (200 OK - with canary):**\n520\t```json\n521\tStatus: 200 OK\n522\tContent-Type: application/json\n523\t\n524\t{\n525\t  \"decrypt_verified\": true,\n526\t  \"hmac_verified\": true,\n527\t  \"last_check\": \"2024-01-01T00:00:00Z\",\n528\t  \"error\": \"\"\n529\t}\n530\t```\n531\t\n532\t**Success Response (200 OK - without canary):**\n533\t```json\n534\tStatus: 200 OK\n535\tContent-Type: application/json\n536\t\n537\t{\n538\t  \"status\": \"unknown\",\n539\t  \"error\": \"canary monitor not configured\"\n540\t}\n541\t```\n542\t\n543\t**Error Response (405 Method Not Allowed):**\n544\t```\n545\tStatus: 405 Method Not Allowed\n546\tContent-Type: text/plain\n547\t\n548\tMethod not allowed\n549\t```\n550\t\n551\t**Headers:**\n552\t- Success: `Content-Type: application/json`\n553\t- Error: `Content-Type: text/plain` ⚠️ **INCONSISTENT**\n554\t\n555\t**Code Reference:** `/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/server/server.go:599-615`\n556\t\n557\t---\n558\t\n559\t#### `/armor/audit` - Audit Status\n560\t\n561\t**Method:** GET\n562\t\n563\t**Success Response (200 OK):**\n564\t```json\n565\tStatus: 200 OK\n566\tContent-Type: application/json\n567\t\n568\t{\n569\t  \"total_objects\": 1234,\n570\t  \"verified_objects\": 1230,\n571\t  \"failed_objects\": 4,\n572\t  \"errors\": [\"error1\", \"error2\"]\n573\t}\n574\t```\n575\t\n576\t**Error Response (405 Method Not Allowed):**\n577\t```\n578\tStatus: 405 Method Not Allowed\n579\tContent-Type: text/plain\n580\t\n581\tMethod not allowed\n582\t```\n583\t\n584\t**Error Response (500 Internal Server Error):**\n585\t```json\n586\tStatus: 500 Internal Server Error\n587\tContent-Type: application/json\n588\t\n589\t{\n590\t  \"status\": \"error\",\n591\t  \"error\": \"<error details>\"\n592\t}\n593\t```\n594\t\n595\t**Headers:**\n596\t- Success: `Content-Type: application/json`\n597\t- Error (405): `Content-Type: text/plain` ⚠️ **INCONSISTENT**\n598\t- Server Error (500): `Content-Type: application/json`\n599\t\n600\t**Code Reference:** `/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/server/server.go:617-639`\n601\t\n602\t---\n603\t\n604\t### 6. Metrics Endpoint\n605\t\n606\t#### `/metrics` - Prometheus Metrics\n607\t\n608\t**Method:** GET\n609\t\n610\t**Success Response (200 OK):**\n611\t```\n612\tStatus: 200 OK\n613\tContent-Type: text/plain\n614\t\n615\t# HELP armor_requests_total Total number of requests\n616\t# TYPE armor_requests_total counter\n617\tarmor_requests_total{method=\"GET\",status=\"200\"} 1234\n618\t...\n619\t```\n620\t\n621\t**Headers:**\n622\t- `Content-Type: text/plain` (Prometheus text format)\n623\t\n624\t---\n625\t\n626\t## Inconsistencies Found\n627\t\n628\t### 1. Content-Type Header Mismatches\n629\t\n630\t**Issue:** Many endpoints return JSON responses but declare `Content-Type: text/plain` in error cases.\n631\t\n632\t| Endpoint | Problem |\n633\t|----------|---------|\n634\t| `/admin/b2/keys` | Error responses return JSON but declare `text/plain` |\n635\t| `/admin/b2/keys/{id}` | Error responses return JSON but declare `text/plain` |\n636\t| `/admin/presign` | Auth errors use XML format, other errors use plain text |\n637\t\n638\t### 2. Format Inconsistency Within Endpoints\n639\t\n640\t**Issue:** Some endpoints use different response formats for success vs error, or for different error types.\n641\t\n642\t| Endpoint | Success Format | Error Format | Issue |\n643\t|----------|---------------|---------------|-------|\n644\t| `/admin/key/verify` | JSON | JSON | Consistent ✅ |\n645\t| `/admin/key/rotate` | JSON | Plain text | ⚠️ Inconsistent |\n646\t| `/admin/key/export` | JSON | Plain text | ⚠️ Inconsistent |\n647\t| `/admin/presign` | JSON | XML/Plain text | ⚠️ Multiple formats |\n648\t| `/admin/b2/keys` | JSON | JSON in Plain | ⚠️ Type mismatch |\n649\t| `/armor/canary` | JSON | Plain text | ⚠️ Inconsistent |\n650\t| `/armor/audit` | JSON | Mixed | ⚠️ Inconsistent |\n651\t\n652\t### 3. HTTP Method Not Allowed Handling\n653\t\n654\t**Issue:** 405 Method Not Allowed responses consistently use `text/plain` format across all endpoints, even when the endpoint normally returns JSON.\n655\t\n656\t**Pattern:**\n657\t```\n658\tStatus: 405 Method Not Allowed\n659\tContent-Type: text/plain\n660\t\n661\tMethod not allowed\n662\t```\n663\t\n664\tThis is handled by Go's `http.Error()` function, which doesn't allow specifying content type or structured responses.\n665\t\n666\t### 4. Authentication Error Handling\n667\t\n668\t**Issue:** `/admin/presign` uses S3 XML error format for authentication failures, while other admin endpoints would use plain text or JSON.\n669\t\n670\t**Admin endpoint auth errors:**\n671\t```xml\n672\t<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n673\t<Error>\n674\t  <Code>AccessDenied</Code>\n675\t  <Message>Invalid credentials</Message>\n676\t</Error>\n677\t```\n678\t\n679\tThis is because `/admin/presign` calls `s.writeError()` which uses the S3 error format, while other endpoints call `http.Error()` or return JSON directly.\n680\t\n681\t### 5. Error Response Structure Variations\n682\t\n683\t**JSON Error Responses:**\n684\t```json\n685\t{\"error\": \"error message\"}\n686\t```\n687\t```json\n688\t{\"status\": \"error\", \"error\": \"error message\"}\n689\t```\n690\t```json\n691\t{\"status\": \"failed\", \"error\": \"error message\", \"result\": {...}}\n692\t```\n693\t\n694\t**Plain Text Errors:**\n695\t```\n696\tError message\n697\t```\n698\t```\n699\tFailed to <operation>: <details>\n700\t```\n701\t\n702\t**XML Errors:**\n703\t```xml\n704\t<Error><Code>AccessDenied</Code><Message>...</Message></Error>\n705\t```\n706\t\n707\t## Response Format Summary\n708\t\n709\t### JSON Endpoints (Consistent)\n710\t\n711\tThese endpoints consistently return JSON for both success and errors:\n712\t- ✅ `/admin/key/verify` - All responses use JSON\n713\t- ✅ `/armor/audit` - Server errors use JSON (405 is plain text)\n714\t\n715\t### Mixed Format Endpoints (Inconsistent)\n716\t\n717\tThese endpoints mix formats:\n718\t- ⚠️ `/admin/key/rotate` - JSON success, plain text errors\n719\t- ⚠️ `/admin/key/export` - JSON success, plain text errors  \n720\t- ⚠️ `/admin/presign` - JSON success, XML auth errors, plain text other errors\n721\t- ⚠️ `/admin/b2/keys` - JSON success, JSON-in-plain-text errors\n722\t- ⚠️ `/admin/b2/keys/{id}` - JSON-in-plain-text errors\n723\t- ⚠️ `/armor/canary` - JSON success, plain text method not allowed\n724\t\n725\t### Plain Text Endpoints (Consistent)\n726\t\n727\t- ✅ `/healthz` - Always plain text\n728\t- ✅ `/readyz` - Always plain text\n729\t- ✅ `/metrics` - Always plain text\n730\t\n731\t## Recommendations\n732\t\n733\t### 1. Standardize Error Response Format\n734\t\n735\t**Option A:** All admin endpoints return JSON\n736\t```json\n737\t{\n738\t  \"error\": \"error message\",\n739\t  \"code\": \"ErrorCode\",\n740\t  \"details\": {...}\n741\t}\n742\t```\n743\t\n744\t**Option B:** Use structured error responses\n745\t```json\n746\t{\n747\t  \"status\": \"error\",\n748\t  \"error\": {\n749\t    \"code\": \"ErrorCode\",\n750\t    \"message\": \"error message\",\n751\t    \"details\": {...}\n752\t  }\n753\t}\n754\t```\n755\t\n756\t### 2. Fix Content-Type Headers\n757\t\n758\tEnsure `Content-Type` headers match the actual response body format:\n759\t- JSON responses should declare `Content-Type: application/json`\n760\t- Plain text responses should declare `Content-Type: text/plain`\n761\t- XML responses should declare `Content-Type: application/xml`\n762\t\n763\t### 3. Unify Authentication Error Handling\n764\t\n765\tChoose one format for authentication errors across all admin endpoints:\n766\t- Use JSON (recommended for consistency)\n767\t- Or use XML (for S3 compatibility, but inconsistent with admin API)\n768\t\n769\t### 4. Create Structured Error Types\n770\t\n771\tDefine error types that can be consistently serialized:\n772\t```go\n773\ttype AdminError struct {\n774\t    Code    string `json:\"code\"`\n775\t    Message string `json:\"message\"`\n776\t    Details map[string]interface{} `json:\"details,omitempty\"`\n777\t}\n778\t\n779\tfunc (e *AdminError) Error() string {\n780\t    return e.Message\n781\t}\n782\t\n783\tfunc writeAdminError(w http.ResponseWriter, err *AdminError, status int) {\n784\t    w.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n785\t    w.WriteHeader(status)\n786\t    json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(err)\n787\t}\n788\t```\n789\t\n790\t### 5. Most admin endpoints return JSON responses\n2. **Plain Text Endpoints** - Health endpoints return plain text\n3. **Mixed Format Endpoints** - Some endpoints return different formats based on success/error\n\n## Summary of Admin Endpoints\n\n| Endpoint | Methods | Response Format | Purpose |\n|----------|---------|-----------------|---------|\n| `/healthz` | GET | Plain Text | Liveness probe |\n| `/admin/key/verify` | GET | JSON | Verify MEK correctness |\n| `/admin/key/rotate` | POST | JSON | Rotate master encryption key |\n| `/admin/key/export` | GET | JSON | Export current MEK |\n| `/admin/presign` | POST | JSON | Generate pre-signed URL |\n| `/admin/b2/keys` | GET, POST | JSON | List/create B2 keys |\n| `/admin/b2/keys/{id}` | DELETE | Plain/JSON | Delete B2 key |\n| `/armor/canary` | GET | JSON | Canary status |\n| `/armor/audit` | GET | JSON | Audit status |\n| `/metrics` | GET | Plain Text | Prometheus metrics |\n\n## Detailed Endpoint Documentation\n\n### 1. Health Check Endpoint\n\n#### `/healthz` - Liveness Probe\n\n**Method:** GET\n\n**Success Response (200 OK):**\n```\nStatus: 200 OK\nContent-Type: text/plain\nContent-Length: 2\n\nOK\n```\n\n**Headers:**\n- No special headers\n- Returns plain text \"OK\"\n\n**Error Responses:** None (always returns 200)\n\n---\n\n### 2. Key Management Endpoints\n\n#### `/admin/key/verify` - Verify MEK\n\n**Method:** GET\n\n**Success Response (200 OK):**\n```json\nStatus: 200 OK\nContent-Type: application/json\n\n{\n  \"status\": \"verified\",\n  \"message\": \"MEK is correct\"\n}\n```\n\n**Alternative Success (200 OK - when canary not configured):**\n```json\nStatus: 200 OK\nContent-Type: application/json\n\n{\n  \"status\": \"unknown\",\n  \"error\": \"canary monitor not configured\"\n}\n```\n\n**Error Response (503 Service Unavailable):**\n```json\nStatus: 503 Service Unavailable\nContent-Type: application/json\n\n{\n  \"status\": \"unverified\",\n  \"error\": \"canary check failed - MEK may be incorrect\"\n}\n```\n\n**Error Response (405 Method Not Allowed):**\n```\nStatus: 405 Method Not Allowed\nContent-Type: text/plain\n\nMethod not allowed\n```\n\n**Headers:**\n- Success: `Content-Type: application/json`\n- Error (405): `Content-Type: text/plain` ⚠️ **INCONSISTENT**\n\n**Code Reference:** `/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/server/server.go:486-507`\n\n---\n\n#### `/admin/key/rotate` - Rotate Master Encryption Key\n\n**Method:** POST\n\n**Success Response (200 OK):**\n```json\nStatus: 200 OK\nContent-Type: application/json\n\n{\n  \"status\": \"completed\",\n  \"rotated_objects\": 123,\n  \"failed_objects\": 0,\n  \"duration_ms\": 45678,\n  \"started_at\": \"2024-01-01T00:00:00Z\",\n  \"completed_at\": \"2024-01-01T00:00:45Z\"\n}\n```\n\n**Error Response (400 Bad Request - Body Read Error):**\n```\nStatus: 400 Bad Request\nContent-Type: text/plain\n\nFailed to read request body: <error details>\n```\n\n**Error Response (400 Bad Request - Invalid Hex):**\n```\nStatus: 400 Bad Request\nContent-Type: text/plain\n\nInvalid hex-encoded MEK\n```\n\n**Error Response (400 Bad Request - Invalid Length):**\n```\nStatus: 400 Bad Request\nContent-Type: text/plain\n\nInvalid MEK length: expected 32 bytes or 64 hex chars, got <actual length>\n```\n\n**Error Response (405 Method Not Allowed):**\n```\nStatus: 405 Method Not Allowed\nContent-Type: text/plain\n\nMethod not allowed\n```\n\n**Error Response (500 Internal Server Error):**\n```json\nStatus: 500 Internal Server Error\nContent-Type: application/json\n\n{\n  \"status\": \"failed\",\n  \"error\": \"<error message>\",\n  \"result\": {\n    \"rotated_objects\": 10,\n    \"failed_objects\": 1,\n    ...\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**Headers:**\n- Success: `Content-Type: application/json`\n- Errors (400/405): `Content-Type: text/plain` ⚠️ **INCONSISTENT**\n- Server Error (500): `Content-Type: application/json`\n\n**Code Reference:** `/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/server/server.go:510-573`\n\n---\n\n#### `/admin/key/export` - Export Current MEK\n\n**Method:** GET\n\n**Error Response (400 Bad Request - Missing Confirmation):**\n```\nStatus: 400 Bad Request\nContent-Type: text/plain\n\nMust include ?confirm=yes to export key\n```\n\n**Error Response (405 Method Not Allowed):**\n```\nStatus: 405 Method Not Allowed\nContent-Type: text/plain\n\nMethod not allowed\n```\n\n**Success Response (200 OK):**\n```json\nStatus: 200 OK\nContent-Type: application/json\n\n{\n  \"mek\": \"64-char-hex-encoded-key\",\n  \"format\": \"hex\",\n  \"warning\": \"This key provides access to all encrypted data. Store securely.\"\n}\n```\n\n**Headers:**\n- Success: `Content-Type: application/json`\n- Errors: `Content-Type: text/plain` ⚠️ **INCONSISTENT**\n\n**Code Reference:** `/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/server/server.go:576-596`\n\n---\n\n### 3. Pre-signed URL Endpoint\n\n#### `/admin/presign` - Generate Pre-signed URL\n\n**Method:** POST\n\n**Request Body:**\n```json\n{\n  \"bucket\": \"my-bucket\",           // Optional, defaults to configured bucket\n  \"key\": \"path/to/file.parquet\",   // Required\n  \"expires_in\": \"1h\",              // Optional, defaults to 1h\n  \"content_disposition\": \"...\",    // Optional\n  \"range\": \"bytes=0-1023\"          // Optional\n}\n```\n\n**Success Response (200 OK):**\n```json\nStatus: 200 OK\nContent-Type: application/json\n\n{\n  \"url\": \"https://...\",\n  \"expires_in\": \"1h\",\n  \"expires_at\": \"2024-01-01T01:00:00Z\"\n}\n```\n\n**Error Response (405 Method Not Allowed):**\n```\nStatus: 405 Method Not Allowed\nContent-Type: text/plain\n\nMethod not allowed\n```\n\n**Error Response (403 Forbidden - Auth Error):**\n```xml\nStatus: 403 Forbidden\nContent-Type: application/xml\n\n<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>AccessDenied</Code>\n  <Message>Invalid credentials</Message>\n</Error>\n```\n\n**Error Response (403 Forbidden - ACL Denied):**\n```xml\nStatus: 403 Forbidden\nContent-Type: application/xml\n\n<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>AccessDenied</Code>\n  <Message>Access Denied</Message>\n</Error>\n```\n\n**Error Response (400 Bad Request - Invalid Body):**\n```\nStatus: 400 Bad Request\nContent-Type: text/plain\n\nInvalid request body: <error details>\n```\n\n**Error Response (400 Bad Request - Missing Key):**\n```\nStatus: 400 Bad Request\nContent-Type: text/plain\n\nkey is required\n```\n\n**Error Response (400 Bad Request - Invalid Expiration):**\n```\nStatus: 400 Bad Request\nContent-Type: text/plain\n\nInvalid expires_in: <error details>\n```\n\n**Error Response (500 Internal Server Error):**\n```\nStatus: 500 Internal Server Error\nContent-Type: text/plain\n\nFailed to generate URL: <error details>\n```\n\n**Headers:**\n- Success: `Content-Type: application/json`\n- Auth Errors (403): `Content-Type: application/xml` ⚠️ **INCONSISTENT**\n- Other Errors: `Content-Type: text/plain` ⚠️ **INCONSISTENT**\n\n**Code Reference:** `/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/server/server.go:828-913`\n\n---\n\n### 4. check B2 credentials\"}\n```\n\n**Error Response (400 Bad Request - Invalid Body):**\n```\nStatus: 400 Bad Request\nContent-Type: text/plain\n\n{\"error\":\"Invalid request body: <error details>\"}\n```\n\n**Error Response (400 Bad Request - Missing Name):**\n```\nStatus: 400 Bad Request\nContent-Type: text/plain\n\n{\"error\":\"name is required\"}\n```\n\n**Error Response (400 Bad Request - Missing Capabilities):**\n```\nStatus: 400 Bad Request\nContent-Type: text/plain\n\n{\"error\":\"capabilities is required\"}\n```\n\n**Error Response (405 Method Not Allowed):**\n```\nStatus: 405 Method Not Allowed\nContent-Type: text/plain\n\nMethod not allowed\n```\n\n**Error Response (500 Internal Server Error):**\n```\nStatus: 500 Internal Server Error\nContent-Type: text/plain\n\n{\"error\":\"Failed to create key: <error details>\"}\n```\n\n**Headers:**\n- Success: `Content-Type: application/json`\n- Errors: `Content-Type: text/plain` (but JSON body) ⚠️ **INCONSISTENT**\n- Note: Error responses claim to be plain text but contain JSON\n\n**Code Reference:** `/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/server/server.go:1246-1324`\n\n---\n\n#### `/admin/b2/keys/{id}` - ARMOR Status Endpoints\n\n#### `/armor/canary` - Canary Status\n\n**Method:** GET\n\n**Success Response (200 OK - with canary):**\n```json\nStatus: 200 OK\nContent-Type: application/json\n\n{\n  \"decrypt_verified\": true,\n  \"hmac_verified\": true,\n  \"last_check\": \"2024-01-01T00:00:00Z\",\n  \"error\": \"\"\n}\n```\n\n**Success Response (200 OK - without canary):**\n```json\nStatus: 200 OK\nContent-Type: application/json\n\n{\n  \"status\": \"unknown\",\n  \"error\": \"canary monitor not configured\"\n}\n```\n\n**Error Response (405 Method Not Allowed):**\n```\nStatus: 405 Method Not Allowed\nContent-Type: text/plain\n\nMethod not allowed\n```\n\n**Headers:**\n- Success: `Content-Type: application/json`\n- Error: `Content-Type: text/plain` ⚠️ **INCONSISTENT**\n\n**Code Reference:** `/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/server/server.go:599-615`\n\n---\n\n#### `/armor/audit` - Audit Status\n\n**Method:** GET\n\n**Success Response (200 OK):**\n```json\nStatus: 200 OK\nContent-Type: application/json\n\n{\n  \"total_objects\": 1234,\n  \"verified_objects\": 1230,\n  \"failed_objects\": 4,\n  \"errors\": [\"error1\", \"error2\"]\n}\n```\n\n**Error Response (405 Method Not Allowed):**\n```\nStatus: 405 Method Not Allowed\nContent-Type: text/plain\n\nMethod not allowed\n```\n\n**Error Response (500 Internal Server Error):**\n```json\nStatus: 500 Internal Server Error\nContent-Type: application/json\n\n{\n  \"status\": \"error\",\n  \"error\": \"<error details>\"\n}\n```\n\n**Headers:**\n- Success: `Content-Type: application/json`\n- Error (405): `Content-Type: text/plain` ⚠️ **INCONSISTENT**\n- Server Error (500): `Content-Type: application/json`\n\n**Code Reference:** `/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/server/server.go:617-639`\n\n---\n\n### 6. Metrics Endpoint\n\n#### `/metrics` - Prometheus Metrics\n\n**Method:** GET\n\n**Success Response (200 OK):**\n```\nStatus: 200 OK\nContent-Type: text/plain\n\n# HELP armor_requests_total Total number of requests\n# TYPE armor_requests_total counter\narmor_requests_total{method=\"GET\",status=\"200\"} 1234\n...\n```\n\n**Headers:**\n- `Content-Type: text/plain` (Prometheus text format)\n\n---\n\n## Inconsistencies Found\n\n### 1. Content-Type Header Mismatches\n\n**Issue:** Many endpoints return JSON responses but declare `Content-Type: text/plain` in error cases.\n\n| Endpoint | Problem |\n|----------|---------|\n| `/admin/b2/keys` | Error responses return JSON but declare `text/plain` |\n| `/admin/b2/keys/{id}` | Error responses return JSON but declare `text/plain` |\n| `/admin/presign` | Auth errors use XML format, other errors use plain text |\n\n### 2. Format Inconsistency Within Endpoints\n\n**Issue:** Some endpoints use different response formats for success vs error, or for different error types.\n\n| Endpoint | Success Format | Error Format | Issue |\n|----------|---------------|---------------|-------|\n| `/admin/key/verify` | JSON | JSON | Consistent ✅ |\n| `/admin/key/rotate` | JSON | Plain text | ⚠️ Inconsistent |\n| `/admin/key/export` | JSON | Plain text | ⚠️ Inconsistent |\n| `/admin/presign` | JSON | XML/Plain text | ⚠️ Multiple formats |\n| `/admin/b2/keys` | JSON | JSON in Plain | ⚠️ Type mismatch |\n| `/armor/canary` | JSON | Plain text | ⚠️ Inconsistent |\n| `/armor/audit` | JSON | Mixed | ⚠️ Inconsistent |\n\n### 3. HTTP Method Not Allowed Handling\n\n**Issue:** 405 Method Not Allowed responses consistently use `text/plain` format across all endpoints, even when the endpoint normally returns JSON.\n\n**Pattern:**\n```\nStatus: 405 Method Not Allowed\nContent-Type: text/plain\n\nMethod not allowed\n```\n\nThis is handled by Go's `http.Error()` function, which doesn't allow specifying content type or structured responses.\n\n### 4. Authentication Error Handling\n\n**Issue:** `/admin/presign` uses S3 XML error format for authentication failures, while other admin endpoints would use plain text or JSON.\n\n**Admin endpoint auth errors:**\n```xml\n<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>AccessDenied</Code>\n  <Message>Invalid credentials</Message>\n</Error>\n```\n\nThis is because `/admin/presign` calls `s.writeError()` which uses the S3 error format, while other endpoints call `http.Error()` or return JSON directly.\n\n### 5. Error Response Structure Variations\n\n**JSON Error Responses:**\n```json\n{\"error\": \"error message\"}\n```\n```json\n{\"status\": \"error\", \"error\": \"error message\"}\n```\n```json\n{\"status\": \"failed\", \"error\": \"error message\", \"result\": {...}}\n```\n\n**Plain Text Errors:**\n```\nError message\n```\n```\nFailed to <operation>: <details>\n```\n\n**XML Errors:**\n```xml\n<Error><Code>AccessDenied</Code><Message>...</Message></Error>\n```\n\n## Response Format Summary\n\n### JSON Endpoints (Consistent)\n\nThese endpoints consistently return JSON for both success and errors:\n- ✅ `/admin/key/verify` - All responses use JSON\n- ✅ `/armor/audit` - Server errors use JSON (405 is plain text)\n\n### Mixed Format Endpoints (Inconsistent)\n\nThese endpoints mix formats:\n- ⚠️ `/admin/key/rotate` - JSON success, plain text errors\n- ⚠️ `/admin/key/export` - JSON success, plain text errors  \n- ⚠️ `/admin/presign` - JSON success, XML auth errors, plain text other errors\n- ⚠️ `/admin/b2/keys` - JSON success, JSON-in-plain-text errors\n- ⚠️ `/admin/b2/keys/{id}` - JSON-in-plain-text errors\n- ⚠️ `/armor/canary` - JSON success, plain text method not allowed\n\n### Plain Text Endpoints (Consistent)\n\n- ✅ `/healthz` - Always plain text\n- ✅ `/readyz` - Always plain text\n- ✅ `/metrics` - Always plain text\n\n## Recommendations\n\n### 1. Standardize Error Response Format\n\n**Option A:** All admin endpoints return JSON\n```json\n{\n  \"error\": \"error message\",\n  \"code\": \"ErrorCode\",\n  \"details\": {...}\n}\n```\n\n**Option B:** Use structured error responses\n```json\n{\n  \"status\": \"error\",\n  \"error\": {\n    \"code\": \"ErrorCode\",\n    \"message\": \"error message\",\n    \"details\": {...}\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### 2. Fix Content-Type Headers\n\nEnsure `Content-Type` headers match the actual response body format:\n- JSON responses should declare `Content-Type: application/json`\n- Plain text responses should declare `Content-Type: text/plain`\n- XML responses should declare `Content-Type: application/xml`\n\n### 3. Unify Authentication Error Handling\n\nChoose one format for authentication errors across all admin endpoints:\n- Use JSON (recommended for consistency)\n- Or use XML (for S3 compatibility, but inconsistent with admin API)\n\n### 4. Create Structured Error Types\n\nDefine error types that can be consistently serialized:\n```go\ntype AdminError struct {\n    Code    string `json:\"code\"`\n    Message string `json:\"message\"`\n    Details map[string]interface{} `json:\"details,omitempty\"`\n}\n\nfunc (e *AdminError) Error() string {\n    return e.Message\n}\n\nfunc writeAdminError(w http.ResponseWriter, err *AdminError, status int) {\n    w.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n    w.WriteHeader(status)\n    json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(err)\n}\n```\n\n### 5. Update Method Not Allowed Responses\n\nReplace generic 405 responses with structured JSON:\n```json\n{\n  \"error\": \"Method not allowed\",\n  \"code\": \"MethodNotAllowed\",\n  \"allowed_methods\": [\"GET\", \"POST\"]\n}\n```\n\n## Testing\n\nTo verify admin endpoint error responses:\n\n```bash\n# Test 405 Method Not Allowed\ncurl -i -X POST /admin/key/verify\n\n# Test 400 Bad Request\ncurl -i -X POST /admin/presign -d \"{}\"\n\n# Test 403 Authentication Error\ncurl -i -X POST /admin/presign -d '{\"key\":\"test\"}' \\\n  -H \"Authorization: invalid\"\n\n# Test 503 Service Unavailable\n# (Requires misconfiguring B2 credentials)\n\n# Test JSON format in text/plain content type\ncurl -i -X POST /admin/b2/keys -d \"{}\"\n```\n\n## References\n\n- ARMOR Source Code: `/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/server/server.go`\n- ARMOR Source Code: `/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/b2keys/b2keys.go`\n- Related Documentation: `/home/coding/ARMOR/docs/error-response-headers-specification.md`\n- Related Documentation: `/home/coding/ARMOR/docs/auth-rejection-headers.md`\n\n## Changelog\n\n- **2024-01-14** - Initial documentation created for bead bf-a5evuz\n- Covers all admin endpoints in `/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/server/server.go`\n- Identified 5 major inconsistency categories\n- Documented response formats and headers for all endpoints","numLines":835,"startLine":1,"totalLines":835}}}
